page: Amazon Web Services g7.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive virtual machine instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance provides 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs, operating with a single thread per core configuration. System memory totals 64.0 GB, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

For accelerated workloads, the g7.4xlarge includes a single NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 GPU equipped with 32 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is bundled with the instance, consisting of an 875 GB NVMe SSD. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 80 Gbps, facilitating rapid data transfers for network-bound tasks.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the integration of dedicated high-frequency Intel cores, local NVMe storage, and a professional-grade NVIDIA GPU provides a balanced hardware profile for resource-dense workloads. The instance is designed for graphics-intensive applications, 3D rendering, video encoding, and machine learning inference tasks that require dedicated GPU acceleration alongside high-speed local storage and high network throughput.
description: The Amazon Web Services g7.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance designed for workloads requiring dedicated GPU acceleration and high-speed local storage. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance features 16 dedicated physical Intel cores running at 3.9 GHz with 16 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory. It includes a single NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 GPU with 32 GB of VRAM, an 875 GB local NVMe SSD, and a baseline network bandwidth of 80 Gbps. This hardware configuration provides a balanced profile for resource-dense applications. The g7.4xlarge is suited for graphics rendering, video processing, and machine learning inference workloads.

tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring dedicated Intel cores, professional graphics hardware, local NVMe storage, and high-speed baseline networking.
